104.reason: getWord returns 0 or -1, never EOF. The loop condition should check for return value != -1 or similar, not EOF.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [getWord returns -1 or 0, never EOF; the loop condition should check for 0 or -1, not EOF]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  getWord returns 0 or -1, never EOF. The loop condition should check for return value != -1 or similar, not EOF.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  getWord returns -1 for both EOF and non-word characters. Comparing with EOF (-1) causes the loop to terminate prematurely upon encountering any non-letter character, skipping subsequent words.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [getWord never returns EOF; it returns -1 or 0. This causes infinite loop or premature exit]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [getWord never returns EOF; it returns -1 or 0, causing infinite loop when file ends]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  fgetc() returns int. Using char can cause incorrect EOF comparison (especially if char is unsigned), leading to infinite loops.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  fgetc returns int, declaring c as char may cause incorrect EOF comparison due to sign extension or unsigned char behavior.
